part_description: Greek terms for peace, silence, rest, and quietness

data_quality_flags.note: Only 2 NT occurrences. Full verse set included.
Only 1 NT occurrence. Single verse included.
Only 1 NT occurrence. Single verse included.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to live in peace, be at peace; to cultivate peace, concord, or harmony
senses: to be at peace, live at peace
to cultivate peace, concord, or harmony
LSJ: bring to peace, reconcile; intransitive: keep peace, live peaceably

verses.text: Salt is good, but if the salt has lost its saltiness, how will you make it salty again? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.
If possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all.
Finally, brothers, rejoice. Aim for restoration, comfort one another, agree with one another, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.
and to esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to be silent, keep silence, hold one's peace; metaphorically to be still, hushed, calm — as the sea
senses: to be silent, keep silence, hold one's peace
σιωπῶν: silent, dumb
metaphorically: to be silent, still, hushed, calm — as the sea
LSJ: keep silence; of bees, to be still; transitive: keep secret

verses.text: The crowd rebuked them, telling them to be silent, but they cried out all the more, "Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!"
But Jesus remained silent. And the high priest said to him, "I adjure you by the living God, tell us if you are the Christ, the Son of God."
And he said to them, "Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do harm, to save life or to kill?" But they were silent.
And he awoke and rebuked the wind and said to the sea, "Peace! Be still!" And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
But they kept silent, for on the way they had argued with one another about who was the greatest.
And many rebuked him, telling him to be silent. But he cried out all the more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"
But he remained silent and made no answer. Again the high priest asked him, "Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?"
And behold, you will be silent and unable to speak until the day that these things take place, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time.
And those who were in front rebuked him, telling him to be silent. But he cried out all the more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"
He answered, "I tell you, if these were silent, the very stones would cry out."
And the Lord said to Paul one night in a vision, "Do not be afraid, but go on speaking and do not be silent,

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to be still, at rest; to live peaceably, be quiet; to rest from labour; to be silent, acquiesce, desist from discussion
senses: to be still, at rest
to live peaceably, be quiet
to rest from labor
to be silent or quiet, acquiesce, to desist from discussion
LSJ: keep quiet, be at rest; resting from war; the dead of night; impose silence

verses.text: But they remained silent. Then he took him and healed him and sent him away.
Then they returned and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.
When they heard these things they fell silent. And they glorified God, saying, "Then to the Gentiles also God has granted repentance that leads to life."
And since he would not be persuaded, we ceased and said, "Let the will of the Lord be done."
and to aspire to live quietly, and to mind your own affairs, and to work with your hands, as we instructed you,

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to be or become silent; transitively to keep secret; passively to be hidden or concealed
senses: to be silent, keep silence
transitive: to keep in silence, not to reveal, to conceal
passive: to be concealed, not to be revealed
LSJ: keep silence; metaphorically of things; transitive: hold silent, keep secret

verses.text: And when the voice had spoken, Jesus was found alone. And they kept silent and told no one in those days anything of what they had seen.
And they were not able in the presence of the people to catch him in what he said, but marveling at his answer they became silent.

But motioning to them with his hand to be silent, he described to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, "Tell these things to James and to the brothers." Then he departed and went to another place.
And all the assembly fell silent, and they listened to Barnabas and Paul as they related what signs and wonders God had done through them among the Gentiles.
After they finished speaking, James replied, "Brothers, listen to me.
Now to him who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery that was kept secret for long ages
But if there is no one to interpret, let each of them keep silent in church and speak to himself and to God.
If a revelation is made to another sitting there, let the first be silent.
the women should keep silent in the churches. For they are not permitted to speak, but should be in submission, as the Law also says.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: peace, harmony, tranquillity; safety, welfare, health; reconciliation with God; used as greeting; from Hebrew — every kind of blessing and good
senses: peace, tranquillity, safety, welfare, health
concord, unity, love of peace, harmony
often with emphasis on lack of strife or reconciliation in relation — peace with God
metonymy: the author of peace (Eph 2:14)
from the Hebrew: every kind of blessing and good
a salutation expressive of good wishes, benediction, blessing
LSJ: peace; treaty of peace; the goddess of peace daughter of Zeus and Themis; Hebraism — greet a person, inquire after health

verses.text: And if the house is worthy, let your peace come upon it, but if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
"Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth. I have not come to bring peace, but a sword.
And he said to her, "Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace, and be healed of your disease."
to give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
"Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased!"
"Lord, now you are letting your servant depart in peace, according to your word;
And he said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you; go in peace."
Whatever house you enter, first say, 'Peace be to this house!'
And if a son of peace is there, your peace will rest upon him. But if not, it will return to you.
Do you think that I have come to give peace on earth? No, I tell you, but rather division.
And if not, while the other is yet a great way off, he sends a delegation and asks for terms of peace.
saying, "Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!"
saying, "Would that you, even you, had known on this day the things that make for peace! But now they are hidden from your eyes.
As they were talking about these things, Jesus himself stood among them, and said to them, "Peace to you!"
Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid.
I have said these things to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world.
On the evening of that day, the first day of the week, the doors being locked where the disciples were for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said to them, "Peace be with you."
Jesus said to them again, "Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, even so I am sending you."
Eight days later, his disciples were inside again, and Thomas was with them. Although the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood among them and said, "Peace be with you."
So the church throughout all Judea and Galilee and Samaria had peace and was being built up. And walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit, it multiplied.
As for the word that he sent to Israel, preaching good news of peace through Jesus Christ (he is Lord of all),
And after they had spent some time, they were sent off in peace by the brothers to those who had sent them.
To all those in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Therefore, since we have been justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
For to set the mind on the flesh is death, but to set the mind on the Spirit is life and peace.
For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking but of righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.
So then let us pursue what makes for peace and for mutual upbuilding.
May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that by the power of the Holy Spirit you may abound in hope.
May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
But if the unbelieving partner separates, let it be so. In such cases the brother or sister is not enslaved. God has called you to peace.
For God is not a God of confusion but of peace.
Finally, brothers, rejoice. Aim for restoration, comfort one another, agree with one another, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
For he himself is our peace, who has made us both one and has broken down in his flesh the dividing wall of hostility
by abolishing the law of commandments expressed in ordinances, that he might create in himself one new man in place of the two, so making peace,
And he came and preached peace to you who were far off and peace to those who were near.
eager to maintain the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
and, as shoes for your feet, having put on the readiness given by the gospel of peace.
And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
What you have learned and received and heard and seen in me—practice these things, and the God of peace will be with you.
And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, to which indeed you were called in one body. And be thankful.
While people are saying, "There is peace and security," then sudden destruction will come upon them as labor pains come upon a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
Now may the God of peace himself sanctify you completely, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in every way. The Lord be with you all.
and to him Abraham apportioned a tenth part of everything. He is first, by translation of his name, king of righteousness, and then he is also king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
Strive for peace with everyone, and for the holiness without which no one will see the Lord.
Now may the God of peace who brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, the great shepherd of the sheep, by the blood of the eternal covenant,
and one of you says to them, "Go in peace, be warmed and filled," without giving them the things needed for the body, what good is that?
And a harvest of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
let him turn away from evil and do good; let him seek peace and pursue it.
Greet one another with the kiss of love. Peace to all of you who are in Christ.
Therefore, beloved, since you are waiting for these, be diligent to be found by him without spot or blemish, and at peace.
And out came another horse, bright red. Its rider was permitted to take peace from the earth, so that people should slay one another, and he was given a great sword.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: peace-loving, peaceable, peaceful; from Hebrew: profitable, blissful; freedom from emotional worry and frustration
senses: peace-loving, peaceable, disposed to peace
from the Hebrew: profitable, blissful
LSJ: of or for peace; of or in peace, peaceful (of life, conduct); peaceable of persons

verses.text: For the moment all discipline seems painful rather than pleasant, but later it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, open to reason, full of mercy and good fruits, impartial and sincere.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: tranquil, quiet; later form for ἠρεμαῖος
senses: tranquil, quiet
LSJ: later form for ἠρεμαῖος; quiet tranquil life; τὸ ἤρεμον — smoothness; adverb -μως quietly
Cognates: Lithuanian rìmti 'grow still', Gothic rimis (= ἡσυχία), Sanskrit ramṇāti 'set at rest'

verses.text: for kings and all who are in high positions, that we may lead a peaceful and quiet life, godly and dignified in every way.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: primarily the relaxing of a state of constraint; relaxation of rigour of confinement; metaphorically: ease, rest, peace, relief, tranquility
senses: relaxation of rigour of confinement
metaphorically: ease, rest, peace, relief, tranquility
LSJ: loosening, relaxing — of strings, opposed to ἐπίτασις; a thaw; of the ebb-tide
LSJ metaphorically: remission, abatement of evils; opposed to θλῖψις; remission of tribute or taxes; of fevers
relaxation, recreation, opposed to σπουδή
indulgence, licence; relaxation of custody

verses.text: Then he gave orders to the centurion that he should be kept in custody but have some liberty, and that none of his friends should be prevented from attending to his needs.
my spirit was not at rest because I did not find my brother Titus there. So I took leave of them and went on to Macedonia.

and to grant relief to you who are afflicted as well as to us, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to make peace; to cause reconciliation between two parties — as in Christ causing the believer's peace with God
senses: to make peace
to cause reconciliation between two parties
LSJ: to make peace; middle — make peaceful of the soul

verses.text: and through him to reconcile to himself all things, whether on earth or in heaven, making peace by the blood of his cross.

word_analysis.meaning_summary: to muzzle; to silence; passively to be quiet, speechless, hushed — as winds and waves
senses: to muzzle
metaphorically: to silence, put to silence
passive: to be silent, speechless
troponym passive: to be hushed — as winds and waves
LSJ: muzzle; shut up as with a muzzle; metaphorically: muzzle, put to silence; passive — to be put to silence, be silent

verses.text: And he said to him, 'Friend, how did you get in here without a wedding garment?' And he was speechless.
But when the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.
But Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be silent, and come out of him!"
And he awoke and rebuked the wind and said to the sea, "Peace! Be still!" And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
But Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be silent and come out of him!" And when the demon had thrown him down in their midst, he came out of him, having done him no harm.
For it is written in the Law of Moses, "You shall not muzzle an ox when it treads out the grain." Is it for oxen that God is concerned?
For the Scripture says, "You shall not muzzle an ox when it treads out the grain," and, "The laborer deserves his wages."
For this is the will of God, that by doing good you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish people.
